title: ED pediatric fever documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.
red_flags.rationale: The exact referral section supports documenting time-critical symptoms, escalation, specialist discussion and handover only when assessed or clinician-decided.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

red_flags.text: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Pediatric virtual-care scope and age applicability were reviewed; in-person examination, tests and treatment remain manual.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

examination.text: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.
examination.rationale: The exact DHA pediatric fever section supports onset, severity or impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, caregiver concerns, risk context, appearance and vital data only when assessed; it does not infer the cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fever in Children

source_limitations: Exact age, measured temperature, pattern, hydration, feeding, urine output, rash, breathing, neurological status, source examination, tests, diagnosis, treatment, dose, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
